# WCPA — Variance-Reduction Benchmark

_Generated 2026-06-07T23:10:05 · 40 replications × 2,000 simulations per method._

Same official-draw simulator, four estimators. **VRF = Var(crude MC) / Var(method)** — a VRF of *k* buys the same precision for ~*k*× fewer runs.

## Title odds — standard error by method

| Team | p(win) | SE mc | SE antithetic | VRF | SE qmc | VRF |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| Argentina | 19.6% | 0.0093 | 0.0105 | 0.8× | 0.0088 | 1.1× |
| Spain | 16.9% | 0.0088 | 0.0073 | 1.5× | 0.0089 | 1.0× |
| Brazil | 14.2% | 0.0085 | 0.0080 | 1.1× | 0.0052 | 2.7× |
| England | 8.1% | 0.0067 | 0.0059 | 1.3× | 0.0053 | 1.6× |
| France | 7.8% | 0.0061 | 0.0064 | 0.9× | 0.0063 | 1.0× |
| Portugal | 6.4% | 0.0056 | 0.0055 | 1.1× | 0.0062 | 0.8× |
| Colombia | 5.0% | 0.0058 | 0.0051 | 1.3× | 0.0054 | 1.2× |
| Morocco | 3.2% | 0.0040 | 0.0045 | 0.8× | 0.0043 | 0.9× |
| **mean** | | | | **1.1×** | | **1.3×** |

## Advancement — control variate (expected group points)

| Team | p(advance) | SE plain | SE +CV | VRF |
|---|--:|--:|--:|--:|
| South Africa | 46.3% | 0.0126 | 0.0060 | 4.5× |
| Qatar | 38.8% | 0.0090 | 0.0046 | 3.8× |
| Ghana | 40.5% | 0.0115 | 0.0070 | 2.7× |
| Scotland | 55.6% | 0.0109 | 0.0066 | 2.7× |
| Bosnia and Herzegovina | 47.5% | 0.0099 | 0.0064 | 2.4× |
| Tunisia | 51.8% | 0.0114 | 0.0075 | 2.4× |
| Sweden | 51.5% | 0.0095 | 0.0063 | 2.3× |
| Uzbekistan | 42.2% | 0.0104 | 0.0078 | 1.8× |

## Read-out

- Antithetic and QMC tighten the **title** odds (mean VRF 1.1× / 1.3×). The champion outcome has high effective dimension — decided by many knockout coin-flips — so the gain is real but moderate; QMC is concentrated on the lowest (final/semis) dimensions where it pays.
- The **control variate** is the big win on group **advancement** (up to 4× here): expected points are known exactly and track advancing closely, so most of the noise cancels.
- All four estimators are unbiased — the point estimates agree within Monte-Carlo error; only the *spread* changes.

_Re-run: `python run.py simvar -n <sims> -r <reps>`._
